tasks.description: Initialize Go project structure: Create 'wt' directory with go.mod, main.go, and basic cmd/ and pkg/ folder structure. Use 'go mod init github.com/bmingles/wt' and add dependencies: cobra (CLI framework), bubbletea (TUI), lipgloss (styling), and go-git (git operations).

Create configuration system: Implement config package that reads/writes ~/.config/wt/config.yaml. Config should store list of projects with 'name' and 'path' fields. Include functions: LoadConfig(), SaveConfig(), and InitConfig(). Config struct should be: type Config struct { Projects []Project }; type Project struct { Name string, Path string }.

Implement git worktree discovery: Create pkg/worktree package that lists worktrees for a given project path. Use 'git worktree list --porcelain' to get worktree data. Parse output into Worktree struct with fields: Path, Branch, Commit, IsPrimary. Create function ListWorktrees(projectPath string) ([]Worktree, error) that returns all worktrees for the project.

Add git status to worktree info: Extend worktree package to include git status for each worktree. Add GitStatus struct with fields: HasChanges bool, AheadBy int, BehindBy int. Add function GetStatus(worktreePath string) (GitStatus, error) that runs 'git status --porcelain --branch' and parses output. Update Worktree struct to include Status field.

Build interactive TUI for worktree navigation: Create pkg/tui package using bubbletea and lipgloss. Implement list view showing projects with their worktrees. Support keyboard navigation (↑/↓ arrows, Enter to select, 'o' to open in VS Code, 'c' to create worktree, 'd' to delete, 'q' to quit). Display worktree info: path, branch, git status indicators (*, ↑, ↓). Use lipgloss for styling (colors, borders). Model should track: selectedIndex, projects, worktrees.

Implement VS Code integration: Create pkg/vscode/vscode.go with function OpenInVSCode(worktreePath string) error that opens a worktree in VS Code. Use 'code' command with worktree path. Handle errors if VS Code not installed. Integrate this into TUI (when 'o' pressed or Enter on worktree). Should work on macOS by running 'code <path>'.

Implement worktree creation: Add CreateWorktree(projectPath, branchName, worktreePath string) error function to pkg/worktree package. Execute 'git worktree add <path> -b <branch>' command. Handle errors (branch exists, invalid path). Integrate into TUI: when 'c' pressed, show input form for branch name, then create worktree in <projectPath>/../<branchName>. Refresh worktree list after creation.

Implement worktree deletion: Add DeleteWorktree(worktreePath string) error function to pkg/worktree package. Execute 'git worktree remove <path>' command. Handle errors (worktree has changes, doesn't exist). Integrate into TUI: when 'd' pressed, show confirmation dialog with worktree details. If confirmed, delete worktree and refresh list. Prevent deletion of primary worktree.

Create CLI command structure with Cobra: Set up cobra commands in cmd/ directory. Commands: 'wt' (default: launch TUI), 'wt config add <name> <path>' (add project), 'wt config list' (list projects), 'wt config init' (create default config). Root command should load config and launch TUI if no subcommand. Add persistent flags for --config to specify custom config path.

Create documentation and installation setup: Write README.md with project description, installation instructions (go install), usage examples, keyboard shortcuts. Add example config.yaml. Create Makefile with targets: build, install, test, clean. Add .gitignore for Go projects (bin/, .tasks/, etc). Document all TUI keyboard shortcuts and CLI commands.
TUI currently auto-expands all project nodes showing their worktrees. Change behavior to make project nodes collapsible and default them to collapsed state on initial display

Border box width is hardcoded to 45 in view.go. For longer project names or paths, text gets truncated. Research TUI widget sizing patterns and implement dynamic sizing that adapts to terminal width while maintaining readability

When user selects a node to open in VS Code, vscode.OpenInVSCode currently opens the folder path directly. Check if a .code-workspace file exists in the directory first. If found, prefer opening that workspace file over the folder

Worktree listing should use 'git worktree list' to discover worktrees instead of assuming they live in a .worktrees folder. The code already uses git commands in ListWorktrees(), but verify it doesn't rely on path conventions for discovery

Worktree creation in CreateWorktree hardcodes destination path. Add ability to specify destination directory while defaulting to current convention (.worktrees/branch-name relative to project root). Update TUI to allow optional path specification during worktree creation

Add category support to CLI and config: Projects currently have name, path, and tags fields. Add 'category' field to Project struct in pkg/config/config.go. Config struct should have 'categories' []string field that defines both available categories and their display order. Update example-config.yaml to show category examples. Add CLI commands: 'wt config category list' (show categories in configured order), 'wt config category set <project-name> <category>' (assign category to project), 'wt config category add <category>' (add category to config.categories list). Category is optional - projects without category field should still work. See existing tag commands in cmd/config.go as reference pattern.

Add search/filter feature to TUI: Currently TUI (pkg/tui/) displays category > project > worktree hierarchy. Add '/' key to activate search mode with text input at bottom of screen. Filter items by matching search term against project name, worktree name (branch), tags, or category (case-insensitive substring match). Display filtered results maintaining hierarchy structure. While typing in search box: all keypresses (including shortcuts) are captured as text input, up/down arrows navigate filtered items. Press Enter to EXIT search input mode while KEEPING filter applied (enables shortcuts on filtered results). Press Esc from search input to clear filter and exit search mode. When filter is active but not in search input mode: shortcuts work normally on filtered items, Esc clears the filter. When no filter active: Esc quits app. Keyboard navigation (up/down) only moves through visible filtered items. When filter changes, keep selection if item still visible, otherwise reset to first. Model struct needs: searchMode bool, filterActive bool, searchInput textinput.Model, filterTerm string. Update buildItems() to respect active filter when building hierarchy.

Display categories in TUI hierarchy: Currently TUI (pkg/tui/) shows flat list of projects. Config now has categories []string field that defines available categories and their display order. Restructure TUI to show three-level hierarchy: Category > Project > Worktree. Categories appear in order defined by config.categories. Projects with a category field are grouped under their category. Projects without category field appear in 'Uncategorized' group at bottom. Each category is collapsible (like current project nodes). Keyboard navigation (up/down) moves through all three levels. Opening (Enter/o) only works on worktree nodes. Creating worktree (n) and adding project (a) work when project node selected. Category nodes display project count. Visual indicators: Category nodes use different style than projects (see projectStyle as reference). Update buildItems() in model.go to build three-level item list respecting category grouping. Store category in Item struct. Add ItemTypeCategory. Handle expand/collapse for category nodes.

Add category assignment feature to TUI with [c] keyboard shortcut. Currently projects can have a category field set in config, but there's no TUI interface to assign/change categories. Implement a flow where pressing 'c' when a project is selected shows a text input to enter a category name. If the category doesn't exist in config.Categories, add it automatically using config.AddCategory(). After input, call project.SetCategory() and config.SaveConfig() to persist. Show error messages if save fails.

Add tag assignment feature to TUI with [t] keyboard shortcut. Currently projects can have tags that are displayed in the TUI (shown with tagStyle in brackets), but there's no TUI interface to add/remove tags. Implement a flow where pressing 't' when a project is selected shows a text input to enter comma-separated tag names. Parse the input and call project.AddTags() for each tag, then config.SaveConfig() to persist. The config already has AddTags, RemoveTags, and GetAllTags methods. Show error messages if save fails.

Add open config file feature to TUI with keyboard shortcut. Implement a flow where pressing a designated key (suggest 'e' for edit or 'C' for Config) opens the wt config file in VS Code. Use config.GetConfigPath() to get the file path (typically ~/.config/wt/config.yaml), then call vscode.OpenInVSCode() with the config file path. This allows users to quickly edit configuration without leaving the TUI workflow. Show error messages if config path cannot be determined or VS Code fails to open.
